intro: The clearest dividing line between CyberArk Conjur and Cycode is access: CyberArk Conjur offers a free tier you can start on, while Cycode requires a paid plan from day one (subscription from $1). If you need to prototype before committing budget, that matters. Beyond the headline, CyberArk Conjur carries high lock-in (enterprise secrets lock-in) and subscription from 60, with a free tier; Cycode carries medium lock-in (supply chain saas, replaceable) and subscription from $1. CyberArk Conjur is a fit when you are teams. Cycode is a fit when you are teams. The honest trade-off: CyberArk Conjur is explicitly not for solo, static projects; Cycode is explicitly not for solo, static projects. On the product side, CyberArk Conjur is described as Enterprise secrets management platform with dynamic secrets, RBAC, and audit trails for DevOps pipelines, and Cycode is described as Software supply chain security platform scanning secrets, IaC, SCA, and SAST across the entire SDLC.
quick_take: CyberArk Conjur is for teams; Cycode is for teams; decide on whether you need a free tier.
verdict_a: Choose CyberArk Conjur if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches teams.
verdict_b: Choose Cycode if its medium lock-in is acceptable for your use case and you are teams.

faq.q: Is CyberArk Conjur cheaper than Cycode?
faq.a: CyberArk Conjur starts at 60; Cycode starts at $1. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from CyberArk Conjur to Cycode?
faq.a: Migration from CyberArk Conjur is harder: CyberArk Conjur is rated high lock-in (enterprise secrets lock-in). You can still move, but expect schema-mapping, export tooling and a longer cutover window. Budget engineering time accordingly.

Both tools tie at 3/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Cycode a good alternative to CyberArk Conjur?
faq.a: Cycode targets the same best-fit audience as CyberArk Conjur (teams). Note that Cycode is explicitly not for solo, static projects, so it only replaces CyberArk Conjur if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (high vs medium), which is the main thing to weigh.
